_note: Human hepatocyte quantitative reference. Every value is measured+cited, an explicitly flagged order-of-magnitude estimate, or null. NOT fabricated. Honesty flags and rat-vs-human provenance are preserved from the source research pass; see docs/12-hepatocyte-quantitative.md for full citations and caveats. Sizes are NOT stored here when not directly measured; characteristic diameters are derived at consumption from volumeFractionPct + countTypical (see engine quantitative/hepatocyte_counts.py).

volumeUm3.note: Wide due to ploidy/zonation; 3400 commonly cited, not consensus.

binucleateFraction.note: Polyploidy (4n,8n) common; per-cell copy numbers likely underestimated for polyploid cells.

membraneDomainSplitPct.note: Sinusoidal (blood/basolateral) ~37%, lateral (cell-cell) ~50%, canalicular (bile/apical) ~13% of hepatocyte plasma-membrane area.

organelles.name: Nucleus
organelles.note: Volume fraction rat (Weibel 1969); binucleate/ploidy human consensus. Characteristic diameter derived ~7-8 um.

organelles.name: Mitochondria
organelles.note: Rat ~20% volume (Weibel 1969); human mitochondrial protein-mass fraction ~23% (Niu 2022) cross-checks. Count ~500-2500, commonly ~1000-1500. Derived char. diameter ~1 um (elongated).

organelles.name: Rough endoplasmic reticulum
organelles.note: Network, not discrete particles; rendered as a volume-correct network/region.

organelles.name: Smooth endoplasmic reticulum
organelles.note: State-dependent: induced by xenobiotic exposure. Model as variable, not constant.

organelles.name: Golgi apparatus
organelles.note: A few stacks/dictyosomes per cell; polarized toward the bile-canalicular face.

organelles.name: Lysosomes
organelles.note: Volume fraction measured (Weibel 1969); count is an order-of-magnitude estimate derived from volume fraction and a ~0.5 um characteristic diameter.

organelles.name: Ribosomes (free + ER-bound)
organelles.note: ~1e7 per large mammalian cell, order of magnitude only. Too many to draw individually; represent statistically (LOD / density).

organelles.name: Glycogen rosettes
organelles.note: Strongly state-dependent: ~0 fasted to >10% fed. Model as a variable pool, not a constant.

organelles.name: Lipid droplets
organelles.note: ER-derived neutral-lipid stores; highly state-dependent (few when lean/fed, dominating in steatosis). ~1% volume + ~100 count are order-of-magnitude for a normal hepatocyte.

proteins.derivation: fraction-of-total-molecules; absolute values exist (Ohtsuki 2012 / Wisniewski 2016) but behind paywall, not transcribed.
fraction-of-total-molecules; absolute values exist (Ohtsuki 2012) but not transcribed.
fraction-of-total-molecules; absolute values exist (Ohtsuki 2012 / Qiu 2013 / Wisniewski 2016) but not transcribed.
fraction-of-total-molecules; absolute values exist (Ohtsuki 2012 / Wisniewski 2016) but not transcribed.
fraction-of-total-molecules; absolute values exist (Ohtsuki 2012 / Wisniewski 2016) but not transcribed.
mass-fraction; CPS1 in top-10 most abundant hepatocyte proteins (28 most-abundant = 25% mass; Niu 2022). Best-anchored of the seven (~half an order of magnitude).

derivation.copiesPerCellFormula: copies = (fmol/ug membrane protein) * (ug membrane protein per cell) * 1e-15 mol/fmol * 6.022e23

ugMembraneProteinPerCell.basis: 464 pg total protein/cell (Wisniewski 2014) x 2-5% plasma-membrane fraction (ASSUMPTION, flagged)

derivation.note: The fmol/ug -> copies/cell conversion is exact; the membrane-fraction factor is the assumption. Drop measured Ohtsuki 2012 / Wisniewski 2016 fmol/ug values into the formula to upgrade order-of-magnitude transporter estimates to measured.
derivation.polyploidyCaveat: Proteomic-ruler copy numbers assume diploidy; hepatocytes are often 4n/8n and 15-25% binucleate, so per-cell copy numbers are likely UNDERESTIMATED (Niu 2022).

citations.weibel1969: Weibel ER, Staubli W, Gnagi HR, Hess FA. Correlated morphometric and biochemical studies on the liver cell. I. Morphometric model, stereologic methods, and normal morphometric data for RAT liver. J Cell Biol. 1969;42(1):68-91. PMID 4891915.
citations.blouin1977: Blouin A, Bolender RP, Weibel ER. Distribution of organelles and membranes between hepatocytes and nonhepatocytes in the rat liver parenchyma. A stereological study. J Cell Biol. 1977;72(2):441-455. PMID 833203.
citations.loud1968: Loud AV. A quantitative stereological description of the ultrastructure of normal rat liver parenchymal cells. J Cell Biol. 1968;37(1):27-46. PMID 5645844.
citations.niu2022: Niu L, et al. Dynamic human liver proteome atlas reveals functional insights into disease pathways. Mol Syst Biol. 2022;18(5):e10947. (human liver proteome).
citations.wisniewski2014: Wisniewski JR, et al. Total-protein-approach quantitative proteomics (proteomic ruler); ~464 pg protein per average liver cell. (Verify exact ref/DOI against source.)
citations.wisniewski2016: Wisniewski JR, et al. Absolute proteome quantification of human hepatocytes (~9400-protein, copies/cell). 2016. (Per-protein tables paywalled; not transcribed.)
citations.ohtsuki2012: Ohtsuki S, et al. Simultaneous absolute protein quantification of transporters, cytochromes P450, and UGTs in human liver (fmol/ug membrane protein). Drug Metab Dispos. 2012;40(1):83-92. PMID 21994434.
citations.qiu2013: Qiu X, et al. Absolute quantification of hepatic transporters including NTCP. 2013. (Verify exact ref against source.)
citations.hpa: Human Protein Atlas, proteinatlas.org (liver, nTPM mRNA + immunofluorescence localization).
citations.uniprot: UniProt Consortium, canonical subcellular localization (ECO-coded evidence).
citations.bionumbers: BioNumbers, bionumbers.hms.harvard.edu; Cell Biology by the Numbers.
citations._note: Citations were assembled from a science-research pass provided by the user. Landmark stereology refs (Weibel/Blouin/Loud) and Ohtsuki 2012 are high-confidence; entries marked 'verify' should be checked against the primary source for exact volume:page/DOI before publication use.
